_comment: Hook examples organized by scope. Global hooks are active in settings.json. Per-project hooks go in your repo's .claude/settings.json.

global._description: These are active by default via settings/settings.json

block-main-commits._description: Prevent commits and pushes on main/master branch

hooks.command: if echo "$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT" | grep -qE 'git (commit|push)' && git branch --show-current | grep -qE '^(main|master)$'; then echo 'Blocked: do not commit directly to main/master' >&2; exit 1; fi
if echo "$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT" | grep -qE 'git push.*(--force|-f)'; then echo 'Blocked: force push is not allowed' >&2; exit 1; fi
if echo "$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT" | grep -qE 'git add' && echo "$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT" | grep -qE '(\.env|credentials|secrets)'; then echo 'Blocked: do not commit secret files' >&2; exit 1; fi

test-reminder._description: Remind to run tests after source file writes

hooks.command: if echo "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE '\.(ts|js|tsx|jsx|py|go|rs|java|rb)$' && ! echo "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE '(test|spec|_test)'; then echo 'Source file written — tests may need updating'; fi

per-project._description: Add these to a specific repo's .claude/settings.json under the hooks key

eslint-on-edit._description: Run ESLint with auto-fix after every file edit (JS/TS projects)

prettier-on-write._description: Run Prettier after file creation or overwrite (JS/TS projects)

ruff-on-edit._description: Run ruff linter after editing Python files

hooks.command: if echo "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE '\.py$'; then ruff check --fix $CLAUDE_FILE_PATH 2>&1 || true; fi

go-vet-on-edit._description: Run go vet after editing Go files

hooks.command: if echo "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE '\.go$'; then go vet ./... 2>&1 || true; fi

cargo-check-on-edit._description: Run cargo check after editing Rust files

hooks.command: if echo "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE '\.rs$'; then cargo check 2>&1 || true; fi
